meta.title: Pluriversal Catalog of Epistemic Tools
meta.short_tagline: A diagnostic-therapeutic map reframing prompting techniques as structural prosthetics for LLM architectural deficiencies.

overview.title: Architectural Repair via Epistemic Tools
overview.intro: The Pluriversal Catalog operates as a structural map for AI-human collaboration. It posits that prompt engineering is not merely formatting, but a method of correcting predictable structural deficiencies in autoregressive token predictors. By utilizing specific epistemic tools, users can bridge the gap between what a model natively produces—greedy probabilistic cascades—and what rigorous reasoning requires—scaffolded, search-based, and metacognitive inquiry.

summary_card.label: CORE THESIS
summary_card.title: Cognitive Reconstruction
summary_card.body: Prompting techniques are piecemeal reconstructions of the human cognitive stack, using token-level context to simulate working memory, deliberative search, and self-correction mechanisms that are absent in the raw model architecture.

key_concepts.title: Core Epistemic Principles

cards.title: Bayesian Evidence Updates
cards.body: Few-shot prompting serves as a posterior probability update, conditioning the model's pattern recognition on a narrow semantic target to resolve zero-shot ambiguity and format ignorance.

cards.title: Linear & Exploratory Scaffolding
cards.body: Techniques like Chain-of-Thought (CoT) and Tree-of-Thoughts (ToT) act as external working memory, allowing models to externalize intermediate tokens and navigate complex logical branching through hierarchical search.

cards.title: Neuro-Symbolic Hybridization
cards.body: Program-of-Thoughts (PoT) delegates arithmetic and procedural execution to deterministic external interpreters, separating semantic parsing from syntactic calculation to resolve LLM unreliability in math.

cards.title: Metacognitive Cybernetics
cards.body: Self-Refine and Reflexion establish closed-loop feedback systems where the model critiques and revises its own drafts, overcoming the generator-verifier gap and stagnant first-pass logic.

key_concepts.intro: The following concepts define the functional layers of the epistemic toolset.

workflow.title: Operational Tool Implementation

steps.label: Diagnostic Mapping
steps.summary: Identify the specific failure mode inherent in the task.
bullets: Determine if the error is due to calculation collapse or logic branching.
Assess if the problem requires real-world data grounding (ReAct) or abstraction (Step-Back).

steps.label: Scaffold Selection
steps.summary: Deploy the structural prosthetic that matches the failure profile.
bullets: Use Self-Consistency for tasks prone to stochastic noise.
Implement Tree-of-Thoughts for multi-path strategic planning.
Invoke Program-of-Thoughts for arithmetic-heavy procedural logic.

steps.label: Metacognitive Closure
steps.summary: Enforce self-correction loops to refine the output quality.
bullets: Apply a Critique-Revision loop (Self-Refine) to the final output.
Check for constitutional alignment if the task involves value-laden content.

workflow.intro: The process of deploying epistemic tools to stabilize LLM reasoning.

checklist.title: Epistemic Tool Readiness

items.label: Contextual Anchoring
bullets: Are there at least 3-5 concrete Input-Output exemplars for few-shot conditioning?
Has a 'think step-by-step' trigger been embedded to activate latent reasoning?

items.label: Logic Robustness
bullets: If the problem is hierarchical, are you using Tree-of-Thoughts to allow backtracking?
If the problem is mathematical, is the model prompted to generate Python code rather than direct answers?

items.label: Metacognitive Verification
bullets: Is there a secondary prompt to verify the output against the initial constraints?
Have you implemented a majority-vote mechanism (Self-Consistency) for high-entropy decision nodes?

checklist.intro: Use this checklist to ensure the prompting architecture is structurally sound.

metrics.intro: Quantitative indicators of tool efficacy in resolving architectural failures.

items.name: Benchmark Delta
items.description: The percentage point improvement on standardized reasoning sets (GSM8K, SVAMP) compared to zero-shot baselines.

items.name: Hallucination Reduction
items.description: Reduction in the rate of factually incorrect or logically inconsistent token generation.

items.name: Schema Adherence
items.description: Frequency with which the model successfully conforms to complex output structures like JSON or YAML.
signals: Structured output adherence >99% with correct conditioning

metrics.title: Success Metrics

failure_modes.name: Greedy Decoding Collapse
failure_modes.description: The model selects a locally optimal but globally incorrect token, leading to a reasoning dead-end.
mitigations: Increase temperature to sample diverse reasoning paths.

failure_modes.name: Linear Chain Brittleness
failure_modes.description: A single error early in a Chain-of-Thought corrupts all downstream reasoning.
mitigations: Use Tree-of-Thoughts for exploratory search and pruning.
Apply Least-to-Most decomposition to simplify sub-steps.

failure_modes.name: Token Overhead Inflation
failure_modes.description: Advanced techniques like ToT and Self-Consistency can cost 10x-100x more in tokens.
mitigations: Use uncertainty-based triggers to only activate tools when needed.
Implement prompt compression (LLMLingua) for long-form context.
